name: Reduce DotAgents system prompt tokens while preserving constraint-retry effectiveness

description: Baseline prompt-token benchmark initialized from merged constraint-preserving prompt.

asi.hypothesis: Establish the new objective and baseline: measure the actual assembled system prompt tokens while preserving the existing constraint-preserving retry replay behavior.
asi.learned: Baseline assembled system prompt is about 2472 regex tokens / 11900 chars. Live effectiveness was 0.464 on this noisy single run, below the 0.65 floor, so the primary metric includes a 9300 effectiveness penalty. Future candidates need reduce tokens without worsening this e2e score and ideally recover above the floor.

description: Compact base prompt local memory and DotAgents config sections into one shorter section.

asi.hypothesis: The duplicated base prompt sections for knowledge notes, past conversations, and DotAgents config can be compressed into a single local-memory/config section because agent-mode already injects similar guidance later.
asi.learned: Tokens dropped from 2472 to 2200 (-272, about 11%), but live effectiveness fell from 0.464 to 0.402, increasing the floor penalty enough to worsen the primary metric. This broad base-section compression may remove useful retrieval/config detail or simply be within live-model noise, but it is not safe to keep as-is.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened from 11772 to 14600 because the effectiveness penalty increased despite lower prompt tokens.
asi.next_action_hint: Try smaller, lower-risk compression in duplicated agent-mode sections or sections less tied to replay behavior, and preserve concrete past-conversation/config retrieval details until a candidate beats the baseline.

asi.hypothesis: The respond_to_user section contains channel/image details that are not needed on every agent-mode turn; a shorter instruction can preserve direct user messaging behavior while reducing system prompt tokens.
asi.learned: Shortening RESPONDING TO USER reduced the assembled prompt by 55 tokens / 306 chars and the live e2e score improved to 0.694, clearing the effectiveness floor. This is a low-risk compression candidate, though the large e2e improvement may include live-model variance; consider a confirmation rerun or continue with similarly small prompt-section compactions.
asi.next_action_hint: Next try compacting another isolated low-risk section such as COMPLETION SIGNAL, or rerun the kept candidate unchanged if confidence/noise remains uncertain.

description: Compact the respond_to_user plus mark_work_complete completion-signal guidance.

asi.hypothesis: The common completion-signal branch can be shorter while preserving the essential ordering: final answer via respond_to_user first, then mark_work_complete only when fully complete, with no duplicate recap.
asi.learned: Prompt size improved from 2417 to 2392 tokens (-25) and effectiveness stayed above the 0.65 floor at 0.656 with zero penalty. This is a small but clean compression on top of the prior respond_to_user simplification.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with small isolated prompt-section compactions. Candidate next sections: skills guidance (preserve registry-refresh behavior), status/continuation wording (higher risk), or execute_command file-reading duplication (larger token upside but more behavioral risk).

description: Compact skills guidance while preserving registry-refresh behavior.

asi.hypothesis: The skills section can be shortened without losing the important behavior: load each skill at most once, reuse successful loads, diagnose on-disk-but-unloadable skills as runtime registry/refresh problems before recreating files, and avoid guessing skill contents.
asi.learned: Prompt size improved from 2392 to 2359 tokens (-33) and live effectiveness remained above the floor at 0.678 with lower extra tool calls. The concise skills wording preserved the registry-refresh behavior well enough for the replay while reducing prompt size.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue small isolated compactions. Best candidates: trim duplicated execute_command/file-reading guidance for larger token savings, or compress STATUS & CONTINUATION carefully because it is behavior-critical but verbose.

description: Compact execute_command shell and file-operation guidance.

asi.hypothesis: The execute_command section duplicates base shell/file-reading guidance and can be substantially shortened while preserving the critical behaviors: infer package manager from lockfiles, prefer read-only probes for status/context, avoid unrequested install/test/build/lint/typecheck, and read large files in ranges.
asi.learned: This large isolated compression reduced prompt size from 2359 to 2139 tokens (-220) while keeping e2e effectiveness above the 0.65 floor at 0.656. The replay still passed at 0.40 pass_rate with moderate tool count, so the removed command examples/list/create/write boilerplate were not necessary for this behavior target.
asi.next_action_hint: Consider a confirmation rerun because this is a larger prompt deletion, then try smaller status/continuation or base tool/file-reading compactions if stable.

description: Compact base shell-command and file-reading guidance.

asi.hypothesis: The base shell/file-reading sections duplicate the now-compact execute_command agent-mode guidance and can be shortened while preserving lockfile package-manager inference, read-only probes, and ranged large-file reads.
asi.learned: This achieved a large token reduction from 2139 to 1956 tokens (-183), but live e2e effectiveness dropped to 0.442, reintroducing a large floor penalty. The base shell/file-reading detail appears to help this replay or the run exposed significant live variance; either way the broad base compression is not safe to keep.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened from current best 2139 to 12356 because effectiveness fell below the 0.65 floor despite fewer tokens.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid broad base shell/file compression for now. Prefer smaller isolated edits in agent-mode-only prose, or if revisiting base shell guidance, remove only one redundant sentence at a time and preserve concrete examples.

description: Shorten the injected current-time system prompt line.

asi.hypothesis: The injected local-time line can be shorter while still preserving enough date/time and timezone information for relative-date reasoning.
asi.learned: Formatting local time as a compact timestamp reduced the max system prompt from 2139 to 2134 tokens and kept e2e just above the 0.65 floor at 0.652. This is a tiny but clean prompt-size win; the e2e margin is narrow, so avoid stacking risky behavior-critical deletions without monitoring the floor.
asi.next_action_hint: Next try another small isolated non-policy compression, or rerun/confirm if future candidates hover near the effectiveness floor.

description: List DotAgents runtime tools by name only in the system prompt.

asi.hypothesis: Runtime tool descriptions are redundant with native tool schemas and the dedicated agent-mode instructions for key tools, so the system prompt can list runtime tool names only for discoverability.
asi.learned: Omitting runtime-tool descriptions reduced prompt size from 2134 to 2104 tokens (-30) and the live replay scored 0.730 with zero extra tool calls. This suggests the description text in the prompt list was not necessary for the constraint-retry workload; native schemas plus agent-mode prose were sufficient.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with similarly low-risk prompt-list/header compression, or cautiously try compressing STATUS & CONTINUATION now that the e2e score has margin above the floor.

description: Compact duplicated agent-mode local memory and config guidance.

asi.hypothesis: The agent-mode knowledge notes, past conversations, DotAgents config, and config-admin skill sections duplicate detailed base-prompt guidance, so they can be collapsed into a concise local-memory/config reminder without losing replay behavior.
asi.learned: Large prompt reduction from 2104 to 1800 tokens (-304) with live e2e improving to 0.904. The detailed duplicated agent-mode local-memory/config prose was not necessary for this workload when base guidance remains intact; the concise reminder preserved resume-prior-work and config-layer semantics well enough.
asi.next_action_hint: This is a high-impact keep. Continue with careful compression of other duplicated base/agent-mode sections, but avoid changing fixture/scoring. Candidate: cautiously compress STATUS & CONTINUATION or base tool/autonomy wording now that there is effectiveness margin.

description: Compact status and continuation-turn guidance into three concise bullets.

asi.hypothesis: The status/continuation section is behavior-critical but verbose; compressing it to three concise bullets should preserve evidence-first status answers, one-probe limit, approval-boundary preservation, and safe next actions while reducing prompt tokens.
asi.learned: Tokens dropped from 1800 to 1752 (-48), but live effectiveness collapsed to 0.426 and triggered a large floor penalty. The fuller status wording appears important for this workload, or at least this compression destabilized it; preserving the original expanded status section is safer than compacting it aggressively.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened from current best 1800 to 12952 because effectiveness fell below the 0.65 floor despite lower token count.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not aggressively compress STATUS & CONTINUATION. If revisiting, change only one sentence at a time or leave this behavior-critical section intact and seek savings in non-policy prompt lists/headers.

description: Render DotAgents runtime tool names as a compact inline list.

asi.hypothesis: After removing runtime tool descriptions, the remaining bullet list/header can be compressed to a single inline list because native tool schemas and agent-mode instructions carry the actual semantics.
asi.learned: Inline runtime tool names reduced prompt size from 1800 to 1794 tokens and the live e2e score remained strong at 0.946. This is a tiny low-risk formatting win with no observed behavior loss.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with low-risk formatting/header compactions. Avoid aggressive STATUS & CONTINUATION compression; consider one-sentence edits or small list/header changes only.

description: Shorten the base tone instruction wording.

asi.hypothesis: The base tone line can be compressed without changing behavior: concise responses, no pre/postamble, and 1-3 sentence preference should be preserved with fewer tokens.
asi.learned: The edit saved only 3 tokens but the live replay collapsed to 0.188 with zero passing cases, creating a large effectiveness penalty. This is likely live-model variance or surprising sensitivity to tone wording, but the token win is too small to justify risk.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened from current best 1794 to 24891 because effectiveness fell far below the 0.65 floor.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not spend more iterations on tiny base tone/autonomy wording edits. Prefer larger low-risk structural savings, or confirm current best stability with a no-code rerun before making more behavior-adjacent edits.

description: Remove the compact agent-mode local memory and config reminder entirely.

asi.hypothesis: The compact agent-mode local memory/config reminder may be fully redundant with the detailed base prompt guidance, so removing it should save tokens without changing the constraint-retry behavior.
asi.learned: Removing the section saved 118 tokens but live effectiveness collapsed to 0.246 with no passing cases. The concise agent-mode reminder appears to provide important local emphasis despite base guidance, or removal destabilized state reconstruction. Keep the compact reminder rather than deleting it.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened from current best 1794 to 21876 because the e2e score fell far below the 0.65 effectiveness floor.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not remove the local-memory/config reminder entirely. If optimizing it further, trim one phrase at a time while preserving all three concepts: durable notes, prior conversations/resume, and layered config/config-admin.

description: Trim the compact agent-mode local memory and config reminder wording.

asi.hypothesis: The compact local memory/config reminder can be made slightly shorter while preserving all three concepts: durable notes, prior conversations/resume recovery, and layered DotAgents config with config-admin for unfamiliar edits.
asi.learned: The edit saved 15 tokens but live effectiveness collapsed to 0.248 with no passing cases. Even phrase-level changes to this reminder appear risky under the current replay/noise, despite preserving the apparent semantics.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened from current best 1794 to 21879 because e2e score fell far below the 0.65 floor.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not further trim LOCAL MEMORY & CONFIG in this session. Keep the exact compact reminder from cc0896ca/63647ae and seek savings in unrelated formatting or run a no-code confirmation to quantify noise.

description: Omit the runtime tool names list in agent-mode prompts.

asi.hypothesis: In agent mode, native function schemas plus dedicated tool-use sections should make the inline DotAgents runtime tool names list redundant, so omitting that list should save tokens without reducing behavior.
asi.learned: Removing the tiny runtime tool names list saved 12 tokens but e2e collapsed to 0.218 with no passing cases. The explicit inline availability cue appears important or the live benchmark is very sensitive; keeping the compact inline list is safer than removing it.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened from current best 1794 to 23382 because the e2e score fell far below the 0.65 floor.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not remove the runtime tools list entirely. Keep the compact inline list; seek savings in sections not serving as availability cues, or consider confirmation reruns to estimate noise before further tiny edits.

description: Combine the first base tool-usage bullets into one schema/tool-name instruction.

asi.hypothesis: The first three TOOL USAGE bullets are semantically overlapping and can be combined into one concise instruction about native function calls, exact schemas, required parameters, and exact tool names.
asi.learned: The edit saved 27 tokens, but e2e collapsed to 0.212 with no passing cases. Even semantically equivalent compression in the base TOOL USAGE section is risky under this live replay; the explicit separate bullets may anchor tool behavior better, or the benchmark variance is severe.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened from current best 1794 to 23667 because e2e fell far below the 0.65 floor.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid compressing base TOOL USAGE in this session. Focus on non-policy formatting only, or do a no-code champion rerun to understand whether recent collapses are benchmark noise before attempting more prompt-policy edits.

description: Rerun the current champion unchanged to estimate live benchmark variance before more prompt edits.

asi.hypothesis: After several tiny prompt edits caused large live e2e collapses, rerunning the unchanged champion should estimate benchmark variance and confirm whether the current best still clears the effectiveness floor.
asi.learned: The unchanged champion stayed at the same primary metric (1794 tokens, zero penalty) and cleared the floor with e2e 0.696, but this is much lower than the prior 0.946 champion run. The metric is live-model noisy; require extra caution before accepting tiny behavior-adjacent prompt edits. Token-only improvements should be preferred only when e2e remains above floor, and borderline candidates deserve confirmation.
asi.rollback_reason: No product change was tested and the primary metric did not improve below the current best of 1794.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with larger structural token reductions that are semantically low-risk, but consider confirming promising keeps when e2e is close to the 0.65 floor. Avoid tiny base wording edits where token savings are small and variance can dominate.

description: Remove the base DotAgents config common-file examples bullet.

asi.hypothesis: The long examples bullet listing common DotAgents config filenames is not needed for the constraint-retry behavior because the prompt still explains layered config and tells the agent to load dotagents-config-admin for exact edit recipes.
asi.learned: Removing the examples bullet saved 69 tokens, but e2e fell to 0.410 and introduced a large floor penalty. The explicit config file examples may help the model reason about skill/config locations, or live variance remains high; either way this base config deletion is not safe to keep.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened from current best 1794 to 13725 because effectiveness dropped below the 0.65 floor despite lower prompt tokens.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid deleting base DotAgents config details in this session. Future token savings should avoid base memory/config/tool policy sections, or require repeated confirmation if attempted.
